what_it_does: A trust-fabric-derived overnight state ledger maintained by the lead session, recording proof status, per-session lane progress, findings, hazards, decisions, and cross-session coordination for the channel-memory mega-prep build.
observations: Uses a three-tier proof ledger: PROVEN-BY-USE, BUILT-NOT-YET-LEAD-VERIFIED, and DOCUMENTED-OR-PLANNED.
Lead role is explicitly verifier/coordinator, not just builder.
Channel layer acceptance suite reported 54/54 pass, including two lead-added restart-coverage tests.
Session-splicing / clone-fleet proven live with both compact and uuid-cut clones.
Noticeboard / board built and reported 26/26 acceptance tests, with a cross-session filing/transition loop.
Heart H1.1 cross-registry resolution/traversal seed reported proven with 11/11 acceptance tests.
Findings section records a resolved CHANNELS_DIR name collision, a concurrent auto-committer hazard, and a watcher death-window race left documented but not patched.
Per-session lane blocks for lead, fork, and recall fork use checkboxes and honest partial markers like [partial] and [~].

Recall fork reports multiple beats: Phase-A dim-mismatch fix, channel-scoped recall (5th wire), crossings graph, distill scaffolding, backfill driver, C1 delegation CLI, MCP recall tool, vault mining, axes lockdown, navigate() address-mover, P0 indexing, and interface bootstrap.
Tim established a standing rule that lead authorization carries the same weight as direct Tim authorization for certain actions.
GPU/resource stewardship is repeatedly emphasized, with staged backfill batches and classifier blocks on heavy passes.
File is truncated near the end, cutting off a line mid-sentence.
standouts: Session-splicing / clone-fleet proven live as the hallmark round-trip validation.
Noticeboard and Heart H1.1 proven by real use.
Recall fork fixed the 2560/384 dim mismatch and delivered channel-scoped recall (5th wire).
P0 substrate-mcp re-index completed ~116K chunks across 10 vaults.
Tim course-corrected to build common knowledge first and appointed the recollection session as Index Chief.
conventions: YAML frontmatter with trust, author, date, and verified fields.
Section headers use stars and emoji to denote priority/tier.
Checkbox lane status per session with explicit [x], [ ], [partial], and [~] markers.
Proof-status tiers are separated from activity checkboxes.
References to git commits, thread IDs, board item IDs, and memory anchors are used throughout.
Decision-for-morning (DFM) references link items to future Tim review.
Honest failure/RED notes are included rather than hidden.
concerns: Concurrent auto-committer hazard swept channel code into a foreign commit, undermining revert-cleanliness.
Watcher death-window race is documented but intentionally left unpatched.
File is very long and truncated, so the tail state is incomplete.
Heavy reliance on external identifiers (commit hashes, thread IDs, board items) without local definitions makes the file hard to parse in isolation.
Some entries mix proven status with future plans, requiring careful reading to avoid misinterpreting intent as completion.
notes: A dense, self-aware coordination log that treats 'looks done' as a risk and insists every claim be backed by a run, diff, or round-trip.
questions: What is the exact content of the truncated tail?
Which Decisions-For-Morning are still open versus already resolved?
What does 'green-paint' specifically denote in this team's vocabulary?
How is the trust-fabric-derived author identity verified?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is an honest morning-surface overnight state ledger; actual content matches, with heavy emphasis on proof tiers and cross-session coordination.
intention_signals: To give Tim and the distributed session team a single honest, evidence-grounded picture of what got built, verified, deferred, or blocked overnight, and to prevent overclaiming progress.
novelty: The explicit anti-green-paint proof ledger with three tiers and the use of live clone round-trips as the validation method for session-splicing.
contribution: A rigorous, evidence-tiered status format that separates activity from proof and records findings, hazards, and honest partials, which a fused system could reuse for transparent multi-session coordination.
summary_for_embedding: OVERNIGHT-STATE.md is a trust-fabric-derived ledger recording proof-status, lane progress, findings, and cross-session coordination for the channel-memory mega-prep build, emphasizing verified-by-use evidence and honest partial states.
intention_for_embedding: To provide Tim and the distributed session team with an honest, evidence-based morning surface of what was built, verified, deferred, or blocked overnight.
